subject: [RFC PATCH 27/40] mm: page_alloc: cross-migratetype buddy borrow within tainted SPBs

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces two new passes in the page allocation process to allow cross-migratetype buddy borrowing within tainted Special Purpose Buffers (SPBs). The first pass, Pass 2c, allows UNMOV and RECL allocations to borrow from each other's free lists. The second pass, Pass 2d, enables non-movable allocations to borrow from the MOVABLE free list of a tainted SPB. This is done to reduce structural mismatches that can lead to fragmentation and wasted space in the system. The patch restricts these new passes to tainted SPBs only, ensuring that clean SPBs are not polluted with cross-type mixing.
